story.iWant: detailed logging output with the --verbose flag
story.soThat: I can troubleshoot problems, understand what parameters were extracted, and see warnings for unmappable fields
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create Logging Infrastructure (AC-1, AC-2) - Create `cmd/cli/logging.go` file - Implement `initLogger(verbose bool)` function with slog.HandlerOptions - Add global `logger` variable - Export `logger` for use by all CLI commands
### Task 2: Add Verbose Flag to Root Command (AC-1) - Open `cmd/cli/root.go` - Add persistent flag: `rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolP("verbose", "v", false, "Enable verbose logging")` - Initialize logger in Execute() based on verbose flag value
### Task 3: Add Conversion Workflow Logging (AC-3) - Open `cmd/cli/convert.go` - Add log statements at each workflow step (reading, detection, parsing, converting, generating, writing) - Add warning logs for conversion errors/warnings
### Task 4: Add Parameter Extraction Logging (AC-4) - In convert.go, after parsing, log parameter count and sample parameters - Implement `countParameters(recipe)` helper - Implement `formatParameterSummary(recipe, limit)` helper
### Task 5: Add Warning Logs for Unmappable Parameters (AC-5) - Identify where conversion warnings are generated - Add logging for warnings returned by converter
### Task 6: Add Performance Timing (AC-6) - In convert.go, track conversion duration with time.Now() and time.Since() - Log completion message with duration_ms structured field
### Task 7: Add Batch Logging (AC-7) - In `cmd/cli/batch.go`, add batch-level logs (start, progress, completion) - Log batch summary with success/error counts and total duration
### Task 8: Add Unit Tests - Create `cmd/cli/logging_test.go` for initLogger() tests - Create `cmd/cli/convert_test.go` (if not exists) for flag and output tests
### Task 9: Add Integration Tests - Test verbose output for single file conversion - Test verbose output for batch conversion - Test normal mode (no verbose logs)
### Task 10: Update Documentation - Update `README.md` with verbose flag examples - Document structured logging format - Add troubleshooting section using verbose logs

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: Verbose Flag Configuration - `--verbose` and `-v` flags add verbose logging mode - Flag defined in root command (global flag available to all commands) - Flag accessible via Cobra context in all commands - Boolean flag (no value required: presence = enabled) - Defaults to false (normal quiet mode)
### AC-2: Structured Logging with slog - Use Go stdlib `slog` package for all verbose logging - Configure slog handler based on verbose flag - Normal mode: Minimal output (success/error messages only) - Verbose mode: Debug level logging with structured fields - All logs go to stderr (stdout reserved for output/JSON)
### AC-3: Conversion Workflow Logging - Log file reading: "Reading input: {path}" - Log format detection: "Detected format: {format}" - Log parsing start: "Parsing {format} file..." - Log parameter extraction: "Extracted parameters: Exposure={value}, Contrast={value}, ..." - Log conversion start: "Converting {from} → {to}..." - Log warnings: "Parameter '{name}' not supported in {format} format (omitted)" - Log generation: "Generating {format} binary..." - Log file write: "Writing output: {path}"
### AC-4: Parameter Extraction Logging - Log extracted parameter count: "Extracted {count} parameters" - Log key parameters in compact format: "Exposure={value}, Contrast={value}, Saturation={value}" - Limit parameter display to prevent log spam (first 10 parameters + count) - Full parameter details available at Debug level
### AC-5: Warning Messages for Unmappable Parameters - Log warnings when parameters can't be mapped between formats - Warning level (not error - conversion still succeeds) - Include parameter name and target format - Explain action taken: "omitted", "approximated", etc.
### AC-6: Performance Timing Logs - Log conversion duration: "Conversion completed in {duration}ms" - Include timing as structured field for parsing - Timing includes full workflow (read → parse → convert → generate → write)
### AC-7: Batch Processing Verbose Logs - Log batch start: "Starting batch conversion: {count} files" - Log progress: "Processing file {current}/{total}: {filename}" - Log individual file results (per-file logs from AC-3) - Log batch summary: "Batch complete: {success} succeeded, {error} failed, {duration}s total"

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: Structured logging with Go stdlib slog (Go 1.21+), log levels: Debug, Info, Warn, Error. Normal mode: minimal output. Verbose mode: Debug level with structured fields. All logs to stderr.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: Verbose bool flag for controlling logging verbosity. JSON bool flag for output formatting (coordinate with verbose mode).

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Requirement: Detailed logging for debugging with --verbose or -v flag. Output includes file parsing details, parameter extraction log, conversion warnings, performance timing. Logs to stderr.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Use slog with structured fields. Log levels: Debug (internal state, verbose only), Info (conversion start/complete), Warn (missing fields, approximations), Error (failures). Example: logger.Info("starting conversion", slog.String("file", path), slog.String("from", src), slog.String("to", tgt))

artifact.reason: Core conversion function that will be wrapped by CLI. Logging will occur in CLI layer before/after calling this function.
Error type that may contain warnings/messages to log in verbose mode.
Data structure for parsed parameters - will need to count parameters and format them for logging.

package.name: github.com/spf13/cobra
package.reason: CLI framework - will be used to add persistent --verbose flag to root command

story-context.constraints: - Logging infrastructure (Task 1-2) MUST be implemented before workflow logging (Task 3-7) - Story 3-1 (Cobra CLI Structure) must be complete to provide root command for --verbose flag - Story 3-2 (Convert Command) must exist for conversion workflow logging - Story 3-3 (Batch Processing) must exist for batch logging (AC-7) - All logs MUST go to stderr (never stdout) to preserve output for piping/JSON mode - Verbose mode MUST add less than 15% performance overhead (NFR requirement from Tech Spec) - Logging uses ONLY Go stdlib (no external logging frameworks) - Global --verbose flag must be accessible to all subcommands (persistent flag) - Normal mode: Level=Error (minimal output), Verbose mode: Level=Debug (all logs)

interface.name: initLogger
interface.description: Initializes slog logger with appropriate level based on verbose flag. Returns configured logger writing to stderr.

interface.name: logger (global variable)
interface.description: Package-level logger instance used by all CLI commands for structured logging.

interface.name: Cobra PersistentFlags
interface.description: Cobra persistent flag mechanism for --verbose flag available to all subcommands.

interface.name: slog.HandlerOptions
interface.description: Configuration for slog handler controlling log level threshold.

interface.name: slog.TextHandler
interface.description: Creates human-readable text log handler for slog writing to specified io.Writer (os.Stderr).

tests.standards: This story follows the Recipe project testing standards established in Epic 1: - Table-driven tests for multiple scenarios - Use of testdata/ directory for sample files (1,501 files available) - Unit tests for isolated functions (initLogger, flag parsing) - Integration tests executing CLI as subprocess via os/exec - Benchmark tests for performance validation (less than 15% overhead target) - All tests use Go stdlib testing package (no external test frameworks) - Coverage goal: >90% for new CLI logging code
tests.locations: cmd/cli/logging_test.go - Unit tests for logger initialization cmd/cli/convert_test.go - Integration tests for verbose flag behavior
tests.ideas:
### Unit Test Ideas (AC-1, AC-2): - TestInitLogger_VerboseTrue: Verify logger level=Debug when verbose=true - TestInitLogger_VerboseFalse: Verify logger level=Error when verbose=false - TestInitLogger_OutputsToStderr: Verify logs written to stderr not stdout - TestVerboseFlag_ShortFlag: Test -v flag recognized - TestVerboseFlag_LongFlag: Test --verbose flag recognized
### Integration Test Ideas (AC-3, AC-4, AC-6): - TestVerboseConversion_AllStepsLogged: Run conversion with -v, verify all workflow steps appear in stderr - TestVerboseConversion_StructuredFields: Verify slog structured format (level=DEBUG, file=, format=) - TestVerboseConversion_ParameterDetails: Verify parameter extraction logged with count and sample values - TestVerboseConversion_TimingLogged: Verify duration_ms field present in completion message - TestNormalMode_NoVerboseLogs: Run without -v, verify only error messages in stderr
### Integration Test Ideas (AC-5): - TestVerboseWarnings_UnmappableParameters: Convert file with known unmappable params, verify warnings logged - TestVerboseWarnings_LevelIsWarn: Verify warnings use WARN level not ERROR
### Integration Test Ideas (AC-7): - TestVerboseBatch_StartEndLogged: Batch conversion logs start/completion messages - TestVerboseBatch_PerFileProgress: Verify "Processing file X/Y" messages appear - TestVerboseBatch_SummaryStatistics: Verify success/error counts and total duration logged
### Performance Test Ideas (AC-6): - BenchmarkVerboseOverhead: Compare conversion time with/without -v flag, verify less than 15% impact - TestTimingAccuracy: Verify logged duration matches actual conversion time
